title: Source vetting + provenance for the exemplar (verify reuse terms; record license status)
context: Provenance and license vetting for the exemplar late-effect entry of ewing-survivorship-late-effects. It enforces the binding cancer data guardrail (open-access/aggregate/de-identified sources ONLY; controlled-access dbGaP/EGA and any identifiable patient data OUT OF SCOPE) and records per-source reuse terms before any synthesis, supporting the no-source-no-claim rule and the fact-vs-expression copyright firewall (synthesize facts and cite; never reproduce protected guideline text/tables/figures). Only open-access/aggregate/de-identified sources are admitted.
objective: Vet and record provenance + license status for every source backing the anthracycline-cardiotoxicity exemplar, rejecting any controlled-access or identifiable source.
acceptanceCriteria: Reuse terms verified and recorded per source (NCI/PDQ public-domain with trademark caveat; COG/ESMO/CCLG/IGHG cited-not-reproduced); only open-access/aggregate/de-identified sources enabled
Provenance recorded per source (body, document, section, version, retrievedAt, evidenceLevel?, url, licenseStatus)
Any controlled-access (dbGaP/EGA), individual-level, or identifiable source is rejected by the allowlist
Reviewed by a credentialed oncologist and the maintainer
